intent: get whisper-writer dictation fast enough and accurate enough for Swedish+English use on my T550 GPU
finding: Across three whisper-writer sessions on 2026-04-16, the user iterates through ~a dozen model+compute_type+vad+realtime combinations manually (e.g. small/medium/large-v3 x float16/int8 x vad on/off x streaming on/off), restarting the service between each and listening for quality and latency. Frustration is explicit: 'SLOOOOOOOW', plus repeated single-word corrections when Swedish terms are mistranscribed. At least ~30 restart+test cycles total. The session eventually pivots to swapping in the OpenAI transcription endpoint, suggesting local tuning didn't converge. The task is: find the best STT config on this hardware. It recurs whenever a new model/hardware/language combination comes up. A benchmark harness — a saved Swedish+English audio clip plus a script that iterates the config matrix, times each, scores each against a reference transcript, and prints a table — would replace the full manual loop once built.
existing_mitigation: None. No benchmark script, no CLAUDE.md guidance on STT config tuning, no saved reference clip.

intent: apply a whisper-writer config change and see it take effect in the running dictation service
finding: Within the whisper-writer sessions, the user issues many variants of 'restart the service', 'restart the systemd service', 'reload', 'config revert and restart'. There is also tray-icon / single-instance confusion where the user doesn't know whether the old instance is still running after a config edit. The task is: apply config change, kill any old instance, start cleanly, verify it's running. It recurs every single time the user edits config while iterating. A small apply-config wrapper — edit config, systemctl --user restart whisper-writer, wait for the tray icon / port to come back, verify only one instance — would replace the manual dance.
existing_mitigation: A systemd user unit exists, but there is no wrapper that ties config edits to restart+verify and no single-instance guard.

intent: keep whisper-writer's transcription initial_prompt in sync with the unusual project names I actually work on
finding: In the whisper-writer config session, the user asks Claude to scan ~/Repos for unusual project names (examples mentioned: autodoro, intender) and fold them into the STT initial_prompt so they get transcribed correctly. This is a task (keep vocabulary hint in sync with repos), it recurs whenever a new unusual repo is added, and a script — a pre-start hook or a cron that regenerates initial_prompt from ls ~/Repos — would replace the manual ask. Only one clear occurrence in this window, but the 'whenever a new repo is created' surface gives it a clear recurrence shape.
existing_mitigation: None. initial_prompt was edited manually in this session.
